title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Automation Case: Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow
summary: Teams copy release YAML across repositories; a shared workflow changes an input silently, accepts all secrets, escalates permissions, and hides which workflow revision produced an artifact. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci permission secret inheritance flow increment in versioned reusable automation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation.
Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow · Theory Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow contract for the supplied data.

For Predict Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow · Theory Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow, keep Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation. A defensible solution applies Ci Permission Secret Inheritance Flow, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned reusable automation contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-permission-secret-inheritance-flow — Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-5
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-5
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: worked gha reuse composite workflow contracts 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
